 {"id":"bd-szi0z","title":"add mounted-path ext4 read-only EROFS coverage for regular file writes","description":"Survey finding: ext4 fuse_e2e exercises writable file creation and overwrite paths, and ffs-core already returns ReadOnly/EROFS for write on a read-only mount, but the mounted-path public write refusal contract is not frozen. Scope: add ext4 read-only FUSE probes that attempt create-via-write and overwrite-on-existing-file writes, asserting exact EROFS and unchanged file bytes plus unchanged directory contents. Acceptance: ext4 FUSE E2E freezes operator-visible read-only refusal for regular file writes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:32:03.961928576Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:26:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:26:00Z","close_reason":"Stale in-progress sweep: no commit reference after more than 12 hours; closed per operator instruction so the work can be re-filed or reclaimed cleanly.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-t6h9","title":"Implement btrfs LZO decompression","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-22T23:43:54.911362121Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T23:58:26.436885896Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T23:58:26.436209759Z","close_reason":"Implemented btrfs LZO decompression with segment framing. Added lzokay-native crate for pure-Rust LZO1X decompression. btrfs_decompress type 2 now parses the btrfs LZO frame format (total_len header + per-page segments) and decompresses each segment. All 3 btrfs compression codecs (ZLIB/LZO/ZSTD) now supported in the read path. 472 tests pass (2 pre-existing btrfs log contract failures).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","compression","parity"]}
-{"id":"bd-t6k9r","title":"[FUZZ-TARGET] add ffs-fuse path-encoding mount-path harness","description":"Apply /testing-fuzzing to the raw path/name boundary in `ffs-fuse`. Build a harness that constructs `OsStr`/raw-byte names with `std::os::unix::ffi::OsStrExt`, then drives `lookup`, `mkdir`, `create`, `rename`, `symlink`, `readlink`, and `readdir` through the existing test doubles and mounted-path helper surfaces. The goal is to freeze behavior for non-UTF8 names, separator-like bytes, empty names, overlong names, and path-encoding corner cases before they become mounted-path regressions.

Primary targets:
- `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:1970-2027` mkdir/rename dispatch
- `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:2244-2445` lookup/readdir/symlink entry points
- current mounted-path helpers in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` that already exercise `OsStrExt` and raw-byte name behavior

Mutation classes:
- non-UTF8 path bytes
- embedded slash / NUL-adjacent byte sequences where constructible
- empty, dot, dotdot, PATH_MAX-adjacent, and rename target/source combinations
- readlink/symlink targets that preserve raw bytes across mount-path lookup and readdir

Acceptance:
- new fuzz target added under `fuzz/fuzz_targets/` and registered in `fuzz/Cargo.toml`
- corpus seeded from current fscrypt-nokey raw-name and rename/readlink mounted-path cases
- malformed path bytes never panic and always classify to stable errno/error outcomes","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:34:30Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T08:34:30Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["FUZZ-TARGET","ffs-fuse","fuzzing","mount-path","path-encoding","testing"]}
+{"id":"bd-t6k9r","title":"[FUZZ-TARGET] add ffs-fuse path-encoding mount-path harness","description":"Apply /testing-fuzzing to the raw path/name boundary in `ffs-fuse`. Build a harness that constructs `OsStr`/raw-byte names with `std::os::unix::ffi::OsStrExt`, then drives `lookup`, `mkdir`, `create`, `rename`, `symlink`, `readlink`, and `readdir` through the existing test doubles and mounted-path helper surfaces. The goal is to freeze behavior for non-UTF8 names, separator-like bytes, empty names, overlong names, and path-encoding corner cases before they become mounted-path regressions.

Primary targets:
- `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:1970-2027` mkdir/rename dispatch
- `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:2244-2445` lookup/readdir/symlink entry points
- current mounted-path helpers in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` that already exercise `OsStrExt` and raw-byte name behavior

Mutation classes:
- non-UTF8 path bytes
- embedded slash / NUL-adjacent byte sequences where constructible
- empty, dot, dotdot, PATH_MAX-adjacent, and rename target/source combinations
- readlink/symlink targets that preserve raw bytes across mount-path lookup and readdir

Acceptance:
- new fuzz target added under `fuzz/fuzz_targets/` and registered in `fuzz/Cargo.toml`
- corpus seeded from current fscrypt-nokey raw-name and rename/readlink mounted-path cases
- malformed path bytes never panic and always classify to stable errno/error outcomes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:34:30Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:47:19Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:47:19Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["FUZZ-TARGET","ffs-fuse","fuzzing","mount-path","path-encoding","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-t97e","title":"Harden ffs-mvcc edge-case tests","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-26T09:56:08.861780486Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T09:59:49.115189214Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T09:59:49.115165159Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-tbn","title":"Implement btrfs transaction model with MVCC integration","description":"# Implement btrfs transaction model with MVCC integration

## GOAL
Implement btrfs transaction boundaries integrated with FrankenFS MVCC, bridging native btrfs semantics with our version-chain model.

## BACKGROUND
btrfs transactions are fundamentally different from ext4/JBD2:
- Transactions are tree-level, not journal-level
- Commit creates new tree roots
- Old roots preserved for snapshots
- This naturally maps to MVCC!

The key insight: btrfs transaction = MVCC transaction boundary.

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Transaction Handle
```rust
pub struct BtrfsTransaction {
    /// MVCC transaction ID
    txn_id: TxnId,
    /// Snapshot for reads
    snapshot: Snapshot,
    /// Pending tree modifications
    pending_trees: BTreeMap<TreeId, TreeRoot>,
    /// Delayed refs accumulated
    delayed_refs: DelayedRefQueue,
    /// New blocks allocated
    allocated: Vec<BlockNumber>,
    /// Blocks to free on commit
    to_free: Vec<BlockNumber>,
}
```

### 2. Begin/Commit/Abort
```rust
impl BtrfsTransaction {
    pub fn begin(store: &MvccStore, cx: &Cx) -> Result<Self>;
    
    pub fn commit(self, cx: &Cx) -> Result<CommitSeq> {
        // 1. Flush delayed refs
        // 2. Update super block with new roots
        // 3. MVCC commit (FCW check)
        // 4. Persist to device
    }
    
    pub fn abort(self) {
        // Free allocated blocks
        // Discard pending changes
    }
}
```

### 3. Tree Root Updates
On commit:
- New tree roots written
- Super block updated with generation and roots
- Checksum tree updated
- Free space cache updated

### 4. Compatibility Mode
For mounting existing btrfs images:
- Read existing transaction log
- Initialize MVCC from latest committed generation
- Subsequent writes use MVCC

## TESTS
1. Unit: Begin/abort discards changes
2. Unit: Begin/commit persists changes
3. Integration: Concurrent transactions with disjoint trees
4. Integration: FCW conflict detection for same tree
5. Recovery: Crash before commit -> rollback

## LOGGING
- Transaction begin (debug): txn_id, generation
- Transaction commit (info): duration, trees modified
- Transaction abort (warn): reason

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. ACID semantics preserved
2. Concurrent writers do not corrupt
3. Crash recovery to last committed state
4. MVCC snapshots see consistent trees","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:51:55.750691013Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T21:33:04.103301376Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T21:33:04.103251292Z","close_reason":"Implemented btrfs transaction model over MVCC in ffs-btrfs with begin/commit/abort semantics, delayed-ref flush, tree-root staging, FCW conflict coverage, and workspace gates passing. Remaining core on-disk wiring is tracked as broader write-path follow-up.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","core","mvcc"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-tbn","depends_on_id":"bd-1b7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:54:13.509360853Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-tbn","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:21.021338413Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-tbn","depends_on_id":"bd-3sj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:54:13.626558426Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":30,"issue_id":"bd-tbn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FEATURE_PARITY mapping: This covers 'btrfs transaction parity' (currently ❌). Maps to transaction.c. When complete: flip to ✅ in FEATURE_PARITY.md.","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:50Z"},{"id":49,"issue_id":"bd-tbn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"BTRFS TRANSACTION MODEL: btrfs transactions group multiple COW tree updates into an atomic unit. Key concepts: (1) transaction handle (acquire on begin, release on end), (2) delayed items (buffered in memory until transaction commit), (3) transaction commit writes all dirty trees atomically. Our MVCC layer maps naturally onto this: MVCC transaction ≈ btrfs transaction.","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:57:59Z"},{"id":110,"issue_id":"bd-tbn","author":"MossyKnoll","text":"Progress update (2026-02-13): implemented  in  with MVCC-backed begin/commit/abort, staged tree-root metadata records, delayed-ref flush before commit, allocation/free bookkeeping, and structured tracing. Added new unit tests covering abort-discard semantics, commit persistence, concurrent disjoint-tree commits, FCW conflict on same tree, and drop-without-commit rollback behavior. Updated  row for btrfs transaction parity to 🟡 with remaining gap note. Gates run and passing: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test --workspace.","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:32:27Z"},{"id":111,"issue_id":"bd-tbn","author":"MossyKnoll","text":"Correction: implemented BtrfsTransaction in crates/ffs-btrfs/src/lib.rs with MVCC-backed begin/commit/abort, staged tree-root metadata records, delayed-ref flush before commit, allocation/free bookkeeping, and structured tracing. Added unit tests for abort-discard, commit persistence, disjoint-tree concurrent commits, same-tree FCW conflict, and drop-without-commit rollback behavior. Updated FEATURE_PARITY.md row for btrfs transaction parity to partial (🟡) and documented remaining ffs-core wiring gap. Gates passing: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace.","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:32:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-tept","title":"ffs-fuse ioctl trace lock held across flush and dropped late","description":"In crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs, dispatch_ioctl_trace_appends_lines_via_buffered_sink holds the trace MutexGuard across the flush() call and until the end of its scope, triggering clippy's significant_drop_tightening lint. While only in tests, this holds the lock longer than needed.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:25:28.751891314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T19:52:43.233319058Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T19:52:43.230422237Z","close_reason":"Isolated ioctl trace flush behind a helper that drops the mutex guard immediately after flushing; verified with targeted rch test and clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Integrate ArcCache with QueuedRepairRefresh + ScrubDaemon pipeline or equivalent.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-13T18:55:03.478585707Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-15T00:39:42.328256059Z","closed_at":"2026-04-15T00:39:42.324578806Z","close_reason":"Wired RepairFlushLifecycle into OpenFs commit paths. commit_transaction, commit_transaction_ssi, and commit_transaction_journaled now notify the repair lifecycle with committed block numbers, allowing repair subsystem to queue symbol refresh per spec §12.1.3.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-w1n6b","title":"[FUZZ-TARGET] add ffs-fuse ioctl argument harness","description":"Apply /testing-fuzzing to the `ffs-fuse` ioctl dispatch boundary. Build a fuzz harness around `dispatch_ioctl(...)` / `dispatch_ioctl_for_testing(...)` that mutates `cmd`, `in_data`, `out_size`, `fh`, `caller_pid`, and read-only/writeable mount options while using the existing recorder/test doubles. The goal is to stress the argument parsers and ioctl response encoding without needing a live mount.

Primary targets:
- `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:1213-1353` FIEMAP parsing/encoding helpers
- `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:1241-1300` move_ext / u32 / flag parsers
- `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:1536-1833` ioctl dispatch for FIEMAP, GET/SETFLAGS, GET/SETVERSION, fscrypt, move_ext, fslabel, and btrfs ioctl cases

Mutation classes:
- short/oversized ioctl buffers
- inconsistent `out_size` vs payload sizes
- garbage command numbers / incompatible flag bits
- malformed fscrypt `_EX`, move_ext, FIEMAP, and fslabel argument layouts

Acceptance:
- new fuzz target added under `fuzz/fuzz_targets/` and registered in `fuzz/Cargo.toml`
- harness proves no panic across malformed ioctl argument shapes
- any crashers become regression tests next to the current unit coverage","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:34:30Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T08:34:30Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["FUZZ-TARGET","ffs-fuse","fuzzing","ioctl","mount-path","testing"]}

The goal is to stress the argument parsers and ioctl response encoding without needing a live mount.

- `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:1536-1833` ioctl dispatch for FIEMAP, GET/SETFLAGS, GET/SETVERSION, fscrypt, move_ext, fslabel, and btrfs ioctl cases

Mutation classes:
- short/oversized ioctl buffers
- inconsistent `out_size` vs payload sizes
- garbage command numbers / incompatible flag bits
- malformed fscrypt `_EX`, move_ext, FIEMAP, and fslabel argument layouts

Acceptance:
- new fuzz target added under `fuzz/fuzz_targets/` and registered in `fuzz/Cargo.toml`
- harness proves no panic across malformed ioctl argument shapes
- any crashers become regression tests next to the current unit coverage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:34:30Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:47:19Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:47:19Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["FUZZ-TARGET","ffs-fuse","fuzzing","ioctl","mount-path","testing"]}

 {"id":"bd-w8amw","title":"Freeze exact TxnId and CommitSeq debug contracts","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T04:51:31.106341415Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T04:56:37.203547933Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T04:56:37.201452016Z","close_reason":"Froze exact TxnId and CommitSeq debug output; verified with focused rch tests plus workspace fmt/check/clippy","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-wch7b","title":"add mounted-path btrfs user.xattr XATTR_CREATE/XATTR_REPLACE errno coverage","description":"Survey finding: btrfs mounted-path xattr coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` currently proves basic set/get/list/remove semantics, but not the operator-visible `XATTR_CREATE` / `XATTR_REPLACE` flag contract that `ffs-fuse` and `OpenFs::btrfs_setxattr` already support. This leaves create-vs-replace errno parity unproven on the public mount surface.

Scope:
- Reuse or extend the mounted-path xattr helper to issue flagged `setxattr` calls on a real btrfs FUSE mount.
- Assert `XATTR_CREATE` rejects existing names with `EEXIST` while preserving the original value.
- Assert `XATTR_REPLACE` rejects missing names with the stable missing-xattr errno (`ENODATA`/`ENOATTR`) and does not create the attribute as a side effect.
- Cover at least one regular-file path and keep the assertions independent of ext4-specific behavior.

Acceptance:
- btrfs mounted-path tests prove create/replace errno semantics and no-side-effect guarantees.
- Any transport-level skips are explicit and distinguishable from backend regressions.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:29:08.677704654Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T10:59:33.040647817Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T10:59:33.038372449Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
